title: coccothrinax miraguama subsp. arenicola
intro: Coccothrinax miraguama subsp. arenicola, also known as the arenicola palm, is a palm tree subspecies native to the coastal regions of Cuba. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The arenicola palm has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and acting as a natural insect repellent. In herbal preparations, the arenicola palm is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, and it is also sometimes used as a food ingredient in traditional Cuban cuisine.
